# Sample fixtures

Two synthetic public PDFs used by the project's tests and the README's
"try it now" examples. They're generated by `generate_samples.py` so
the content is fully reproducible and doesn't include any private
documents.

| File | Bytes | Code path it exercises |
|---|---|---|
| `text_with_tables.pdf` | ~7 KB | pdf2docx text extraction, lattice table detection, first-table-header re-injection |
| `scanned_text.pdf` | ~68 KB | Tesseract OCR fallback (`ocr_to_docx`) — pdf2docx sees zero words on this file |

## Quick demo

```bash
# Text path (no OCR needed)
python src/pdf_to_docx.py tests/data/text_with_tables.pdf out.docx

# OCR path
python src/pdf_to_docx.py tests/data/scanned_text.pdf out.docx --ocr --ocr-language eng
```

## Regenerating

The fixtures only need to be regenerated when you've changed the
generator script itself. Otherwise the committed copies are fine.

```bash
pip install Pillow      # generator's only extra dependency
python tests/data/generate_samples.py
```

The output is deterministic. Both files have placeholder content only
("Sample Document", "alpha/beta/gamma" table cells, etc.) and are
safe to publish.
